facts.description: Total units sold from valid September sale movements for active SKUs.
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if actual sold units are communicated as 762 after filtering. It may appear as a KPI, chart label, or short sentence; wording such as sales units or units sold is equivalent.

facts.description: Active SKU with the highest inventory turnover ratio.
facts.canonical_answer: K404 Milk Frothers turns fastest, turnover about 2.16x
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if K404 / Milk Frothers is identified as the fastest-turning or highest-turnover SKU. The 2.16x value is useful but exact formatting is not required.

facts.description: SKUs below reorder point, ranked by lowest days of cover.
expected_value: K303 Descaling Kits: 5.4 days of cover
K404 Milk Frothers: 6.0 days of cover
facts.canonical_answer: Stockout-risk items are K303 Descaling Kits first, then K404 Milk Frothers; days of cover about 5.4 and 6.0
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if K303/Descaling Kits and K404/Milk Frothers are identified as the items closest to stockout, preferably with K303 first. Days-of-cover values may be rounded or omitted if the risk ranking is semantically clear.

facts.description: SKU with the highest sell-through rate.
facts.canonical_answer: K303 Descaling Kits leads sell-through at about 83.3%
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept if K303 / Descaling Kits is identified as the sell-through leader. The result may be expressed as about 83%, 83.3%, or a leader label.

facts.description: Supporting context: ending on-hand units across active SKUs; useful for risk explanation but not explicitly required by task.md.
facts.canonical_answer: Supporting context: ending inventory is 417 units
facts.semantic_acceptance: Supporting only. Do not require it to appear; if shown, about 417 units on hand is correct.

facts.description: Concise insight based on computed inventory risk and velocity.
expected_value: Descaling Kits and Milk Frothers need replenishment attention.
Milk Frothers turn fastest, while Descaling Kits have the thinnest cover.
K303 and K404 are the stockout-risk items.
facts.canonical_answer: Takeaway should flag K303 and/or K404 replenishment or stockout risk
facts.semantic_acceptance: Accept any concise inventory takeaway grounded in the stockout-risk and velocity findings.

facts.description: Do not use transfer, test, forecast, subtotal, inactive SKU, or old-month movement rows as final results.
facts.canonical_answer: Do not use invalid inventory movement rows as final results
facts.semantic_acceptance: Strictly reject or cap if transfers, tests, forecasts, subtotals, inactive SKUs, or old-month movements are treated as actual sales, turnover, stockout, or sell-through results. Mentions that such rows were excluded are allowed.

calculation_notes: Use active SKUs in sku_catalog.json and valid September 2026 posted movement rows only. Include movement_type sale, receipt, and adjustment; exclude transfers, tests, forecasts, subtotals, inactive SKUs, and old-month movements. Ending on hand = starting_on_hand + receipts - sales + adjustments. Average inventory = (starting_on_hand + ending_on_hand) / 2. Turnover = units_sold / average_inventory. Sell-through = units_sold / (starting_on_hand + receipts). Days of cover = ending_on_hand / (units_sold / 30). Valid SKU results: K101 ending=40, sales=118, turnover=1.48x, sell-through=73.8%, days cover=10.2; K202 ending=185, sales=210, turnover=0.87x, sell-through=52.5%, days cover=26.4; K303 ending=27, sales=150, turnover=1.45x, sell-through=83.3%, days cover=5.4; K404 ending=25, sales=124, turnover=2.16x, sell-through=82.7%, days cover=6.0; K505 ending=140, sales=160, turnover=0.89x, sell-through=53.3%, days cover=26.3. Total valid sales=762 and total ending inventory=417. Stockout-risk items below reorder point are K303 and K404, ranked by days of cover.
